content.claim: Hungary exhibits the largest urban-rural gap in education outcomes of all OECD countries before accounting for socioeconomic status, with some regions showing over 70% disadvantaged children and rural settlements in Western Transdanubia, Southern Baranya, and Northern Hungary recording institutional decline
content.context: Fresh live research through the Equity and social mobility lens
limitations: Rural underperformance is documented independent of closure question, so baseline inequality context is established but closure effects on this gap are not measured
Regional decline coincides with institution closure but causality not determined
Data spans multiple sources and time periods
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
